entities.name: VS_Category
observations: VS = Vertical Slice business domains (VS0-VS7): VS0 represents the Shared Kernel foundation layer (not a business vertical slice), including SK_ENV/SK_AUTH_SNAP contracts and core infrastructure ports (IAuthService, IFirestoreRepo) used by all slices; VS1-VS7 are the actual business vertical-slice domains (identity, account, skill-xp, organization, workspace, scheduling, notification).
Cross-slice collaboration must occur through contracts/events/projections; no direct aggregate mutation across slices.
Detailed per-slice flow remains in docs/logic-overview.md (SSOT).

entities.name: L_Category
observations: L = Layer runtime pipeline: L0 triggers → L1 shared-kernel → L2 command gateway → L3 slices → L4 IER → L5 projection bus → L6 query gateway → L7 Firebase ACL → L8 Firebase infra → L9 observability.
L5 ProjectionBus and L9 Observability are infrastructure layers (no VS designation).
Layer responsibilities are orchestration boundaries; do not bypass contracts.

entities.name: R_Category
observations: R = runtime invariants: single traceId injection at CBG_ENTRY, read-only propagation, deterministic event envelope fields, version-safe projection updates.
Projection writes must apply monotonic version guard (aggregateVersion).
Runtime invariants are non-optional and validated via architecture rules.

entities.name: S_Category
observations: S = shared contracts from Shared Kernel: outbox relay, version guard, staleness/read consistency, resilience/retry, token refresh, and port boundaries.
All projections must remain replay/rebuild safe via event-store driven reconstruction.
Skill tier semantics stay pure-function based (getTier(xp)); never persisted as mutable source-of-truth.

entities.name: A_Category
observations: A = architecture invariants + SSOT indexes: #N indexes cross-cutting consistency/design principles from docs/logic-overview.md (example: #9 replay rebuildability), while A# indexes atomicity/transaction-boundary invariants (example: A8 one-command/one-aggregate TX atomicity).

Critical retained references: #9 replay rebuildability, #12 getTier(xp) purity, #14 scheduling reads ORG_ELIGIBLE_MEMBER_VIEW, #19 projection monotonic version guard, A8 one-command/one-aggregate TX atomicity, A9 scope-guard re-source for high risk.
For detailed contract behavior, layer responsibilities, and VS1-VS7 implementation flows, consult docs/logic-overview.md as SSOT when compressed entries are not sufficient.

entities.name: D_Category
observations: D = design/governance rules D1-D25; full text remains in docs/logic-overview.md and lint architecture checks.

All D-rules (D1-D25) are mandatory. High-risk/frequently violated boundaries called out for enforcement focus: D7 public slice boundary via {slice}/index.ts, D24 no direct firebase/* imports in feature slices, D25 new Firebase capability requires FIREBASE_ACL adapter + SK_PORTS alignment; D24+D25 jointly define Firebase isolation boundary strategy.
D-rules are evolvable governance but cannot violate R/S/A/# hard invariants.

entities.name: P_Category
observations: P = processing/policy rules for command orchestration, saga/pipeline handling, and protection against unsafe replay paths.
Policy execution must preserve auditability, idempotency, and eventual consistency guarantees.
Queue/background lanes may optimize throughput but cannot bypass contract checks.

entities.name: T_Category
observations: T = tag semantics represented in this compressed model by T_Category, sourced from the standalone TAG_AUTH subgraph in docs/logic-overview.md (Tag Authority subsection + D21 governance section).
Architectural clarification: TAG_AUTH is outside Shared Kernel while retaining centralized authority via D21 and cross-slice projection consumption.
Critical retained references: T4 scheduling demand uses SK_SKILL_REQ × tagSlug, T5 TAG_SNAPSHOT is read-only for consumers.

entities.name: E_Category
observations: E = event semantics: domain events flow through outbox/IER/projection bus; no direct side-channel delivery.
EventEnvelope requires causationId + correlationId for traceable cross-slice processing.
Event semantics must support deterministic replay and projection rebuild.
